title: OpenAI Launches Enterprise Platform for Seamless AI Integration
full_text:
OpenAI Launches Enterprise Platform for Seamless AI Integration
This title was summarized by AI from the post below.
AI Business
43,605 followers
4mo
Report this post
OpenAI's Latest Platform Targets Enterprise Customers: The company said it developed the platform to address a growing enterprise challenge: While individual AI agents improve efficiency, they need to work more effectively across the business.

Yay, #consulting is so back! 🔄🎉
Step 1: Build AI to disrupt consultants. 🤖
Step 2: Hire consultants to sell the #AI . 💼
The peak irony of this entire cycle is just delicious! 🤌✨
Accenture , McKinsey & Company , Boston Consulting Group (BCG) , Capgemini
TechCrunch
2,978,126 followers
4mo
OpenAI is partnering with four consulting giants in an effort to see more adoption of its OpenAI Frontier AI agent platform.
Like
Comment
Share
Copy
LinkedIn
Facebook
To view or add a comment, sign in
Adam Harris
4mo
Edited
Report this post
The major consulting firms are doubling down on OpenAI — and that's a good thing for the market.
But there's still a huge gap.
Countless businesses want the same incredible advantages that come from deploying OpenAI's technology well without the cost, complexity, and timelines of traditional enterprise programs.
That's why Alex Owens and I started CHIEFTNS . Tools such as ChatGPT Business are available to all but deploying them well is where the compounding value really lies.
https://lnkd.in/gwFB8ErM

Like
Comment
Share
Copy
LinkedIn
Facebook
To view or add a comment, sign in
César Beltrán Miralles
4mo
Report this post
Great perspective about OpenAI's new Frontier platform. I love the distinction between 'Onboarding' and 'Performance Review.'
While Frontier solves the Identity and Context silos, it doesn't solve the Execution gap. Without that layer of Process Intelligence, we risk having highly capable agents operating in a black box.
The governance piece, ensuring compliance and preventing rework, is critical for enterprise adoption. We need that loop to distinguish between simple activity and actual value.
Process Intelligence is non-negotiable for ensuring and measuring the performance (effectiveness and efficiency) of the digital workforce.
#ProcessIntelligence #Celonis #EnterpriseAI
Steve Carpenter
30 Years in the Trenches at Merck, JCI, & GE | Architecting 2026 Operating Models to Bridge the Value Gap
4mo
Edited
I recently read an article on OpenAI’s new Frontier platform, which prompted me to reflect on the direction of the ecosystem.
https://lnkd.in/g_Y3dd9G
Frontier represents a significant advancement. It addresses the identity problem and establishes a "Shared Business Context," enabling agents to understand what your business does.
However, knowing what to do is not the same as executing it efficiently.
Frontier equips agents with Knowledge (Data, Docs, Permissions), while the Process Intelligence ecosystem provides the Wisdom of Execution (Flow, Bottlenecks, Compliance).
If Frontier facilitates the onboarding of the agent, Process Intelligence oversees the Performance Review by addressing questions such as:
- Are they taking the long way around?
- Are they creating rework downstream?
- Are they compliant with the actual process?
Frontier manages the Agent, while Process Intelligence manages the Value. Tell me what you think.
#ProcessIntelligence #AI #FutureOfWork #DigitalTransformation

Great perspective about OpenAI's new Frontier platform. I love the distinction between 'Onboarding' and 'Performance Review.'
While Frontier solves the Identity and Context silos, it doesn't solve the Execution gap. Without that layer of Process Intelligence, we risk having highly capable agents operating in a black box.
The governance piece, ensuring compliance and preventing rework, is critical for enterprise adoption. We need that loop to distinguish between simple activity and actual value.
Process Intelligence is non-negotiable for ensuring and measuring the performance (effectiveness and efficiency) of the digital workforce.
#ProcessIntelligence #Celonis #EnterpriseAI

I recently read an article on OpenAI’s new Frontier platform, which prompted me to reflect on the direction of the ecosystem.
https://lnkd.in/g_Y3dd9G
Frontier represents a significant advancement. It addresses the identity problem and establishes a "Shared Business Context," enabling agents to understand what your business does.
However, knowing what to do is not the same as executing it efficiently.
Frontier equips agents with Knowledge (Data, Docs, Permissions), while the Process Intelligence ecosystem provides the Wisdom of Execution (Flow, Bottlenecks, Compliance).
If Frontier facilitates the onboarding of the agent, Process Intelligence oversees the Performance Review by addressing questions such as:
- Are they taking the long way around?
- Are they creating rework downstream?
- Are they compliant with the actual process?
Frontier manages the Agent, while Process Intelligence manages the Value. Tell me what you think.
#ProcessIntelligence #AI #FutureOfWork #DigitalTransformation
